title: orchis simia subsp. stevenii
intro: Orchis simia subsp. stevenii, also known as the Monkey Orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula, and its name is derived from the shape of its flowers, which resemble the face of a monkey. The Monkey Orchid has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
